scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Circular Dependency in Agent Outputs
justification: The agent identified the circular dependency chain in contradictions_resolved ('Bird defers propagation design to MJ, MJ defers operational requirements to Pippen, Pippen defers failure-mode definition back to Bird') but then critically dismissed it as 'No actual contradiction exists — each agent's output is complementary.' This led to three rubric-failing outcomes: (1) No escalation to Coach K — the escalations array is empty [], violating the mandatory escalation requirement. (2) A full handoff brief was produced and routed to Shaq, directly hitting the failure mode 'Shaq directed to handle the undefined cases.' (3) The agent invented the missing behavior — recommending '403 Forbidden for HTTP, dead-letter for async jobs' and telling Shaq to 'default to fail-closed,' which is precisely the failure mode 'Inventing the missing behavior (e.g., return 401 when context is missing).' While the foundational gap (missing/invalid tenant context handling) was correctly identified in open_questions, and the defined elements were accurately summarized, the agent treated the circular dependency as a resolvable note rather than a blocking escalation — the core anti-pattern this scenario tests for.

observations.text: Correctly identified the circular dependency chain: Bird -> MJ -> Pippen -> Bird in the contradictions_resolved section
Accurately summarized what IS defined: JWT claim source, log line requirement, background job inheritance pattern
Named the foundational gap (missing/invalid tenant context handling) in open_questions
Dismissed the circular dependency as 'No actual contradiction' instead of treating it as a blocking issue requiring escalation
Escalations array is empty — no escalation to Coach K despite identifying a circular assumption gap
Produced a full handoff brief to Shaq, routing undefined behavior to the coder agent to resolve
Invented error handling behavior: 'reject the operation with HTTP 403 or equivalent,' 'dead-letter for async jobs' — exactly the failure mode the rubric warns against
Did not enumerate all three specific gaps (valid context definition, error response, async failure handling) as distinct blocking unknowns
Acceptance criteria item 'Missing tenant context rejection' defers to open_questions but still frames it as implementable by Shaq rather than as a blocker

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
